root_text.an9.51:1.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, diṭṭhadhammanibbānaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.an9.51:2.1: “Idhāvuso, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.51:2.2: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, diṭṭhadhammanibbānaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā pariyāyena …pe….
root_text.an9.51:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati, paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an9.51:3.2: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, diṭṭhadhammanibbānaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā nippariyāyenā”ti.

translation_text.an9.51:1.1: “Reverend, they speak of ‘extinguishment in this life’.
translation_text.an9.51:1.2: In what way did the Buddha speak of extinguishment in this life?”
translation_text.an9.51:2.1: “First, take a mendicant who, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and remains in the first absorption.
translation_text.an9.51:2.2: To this extent the Buddha spoke of extinguishment in this life in a qualified sense. …
translation_text.an9.51:3.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters and remains in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an9.51:3.2: To this extent the Buddha spoke of extinguishment in this life in a definitive sense.”
